questions.question: What is the preferred approach used by Prometheus to collect metrics from a target?
What is the Observability?
questions.answer: Observability: understand what’s happening inside a system and predict how it will behave in the future

questions.question: What is RED Method?
questions.answer: RED Method consists of: (Request) Rate + (Request) Errors + (Request) Duration

questions.question: What are the distinctions between SLO, SLI, and SLA?
questions.answer: SLO: Service Level Objective (Goal), SLA: Service Level Agreement (Contract), SLI: Service Level Indicator (Metrics)

questions.question: In the context of tracing, what is the meaning or representation of a span?
questions.answer: Span is a single operation/unit of work within a distributed system and captures the start and end times, duration, and associated metadata of a specific operation

questions.question: In which scenarios is distributed tracing less beneficial or NOT as applicable?
What are typically tracked within a span of a trace?
questions.answer: Operation Name, Trace ID and Span ID, Start and End Timestamps, Duration, Parent Span ID

questions.question: What is the good and bad metric?
questions.answer: bad: a metric with a lot fo variance and poor correlation with user experience, good: metric to set easier threashold for bcs there is no overlap at all.

questions.question: Which type of data is monitored by Prometheus?
In the context of monitoring and observability, what type of data is typically used to define a SLI?
questions.answer: SLI is typically derived from metrics

questions.question: What is the meaning or purpose of an error budget policy?
questions.answer: An error budget policy is a concept used in the context of SLOs and SLAs and is to define the acceptable level of errors or service disruptions that a system or service can experience within a given time period.

questions.question: What is one advantage of the push model for recoring metrics compared to pull models?
questions.answer: timely and proactive data collection (real-time or near real-time) / pushing into the centralized data system

questions.question: How do Prometheus, ELK stack, and InfluxDB differ in terms of their functionalities and use cases?
questions.answer: InfluxDB is a pull-based time-series database designed to handle high volumes of time-stamped data (IoT, Sensor, Analytics).

questions.question: What is the definition of a metric?
questions.answer: ELK stack is a push-based system, used for collecting, processing, storing, and visualizing log data.

questions.question: What are the Prometheus exemplars?
questions.answer: Prometheus is a pull-based time-series database and monitoring system specifically designed for monitoring dynamic cloud-native environments.

questions.question: What is one of the main purposes or goals of logging?
What are the 3 core components of observability?
questions.answer: An exemplar is a specific trace representative of measurement taken in a given time interval and provides additional information about a specific data point.

questions.question: What is the Monitoring
questions.answer: To gather and aggregate textual event data from a service for troubleshooting

questions.question: What is the Telemetry?
What is the challenges of observability?
questions.answer: Monitoring: continues observation of a system to detect and alert on abnormal behavior.

questions.question: What is the CLI utility tool for Prometheus called?
What are the limitations of Prometheus?
questions.answer: scalability for large-scale deployments with millions of TS, Long-term storage, High cardinality, HA and Replication

questions.question: What is Service Discovery and which categories are there?
questions.answer: SD is a mechanism that allow to automatically discover and monitor targets and services. There are 2 categories: top-down (e.i. ec2) and bottom-up (e.i. consol) mechanisms of static SD

questions.question: Which property configures the timing to scrape metrics from targets?
Which section in the Prometheus configuration file governs the selection of targets to be scraped?
Which action in the label configuration is used to delete a specific target?
questions.answer: scrape_configs -> relabel_configs -> action: drop or action: keep

questions.question: How is managed data retention in prometheus?
What are the essential 3 components of Prometheus?
What is required to be able to reload Prometheus?
What are 3 methods to restart the Prometheus server?
questions.answer: Sending a SIGHUP signal to the Prometheus process, Using the Prometheus API POST or PUT + /-/reload, Using a service manager (systemctl) or orchestration tool (k8s)

questions.question: What HTTP method does Prometheus employ for performing scrapes?
Which SD configuration is recommended for scraping EC2 instances?
Which SD configuration is recommended for nodes of Elastic Kubernetes Service on AWS?
What is the purpose of the scrape_interval configuration in Prometheus?
questions.answer: how frequently Prometheus collects and updates the metrics

questions.question: Which type of database does Prometheus utilize?
What component is responsible for collecting metrics from an instance and exposing them in a format that Prometheus expects?
Which component is suitable for collecting metrics from batch/cron jobs?
When is the configuration option honor_labels:true used?
questions.answer: Using honor_labels can make your collected metrics more informative and allow you to differentiate between different metrics coming from various sources or probe targets

questions.question: What is the purpose of port 9090/9093/9100/9091/9115 in Prometheus?
what are 2 default metric labels?
Which of the file systems is recommended/supported by Prometheus?
How can you configure a Blackbox Exporter probe to check the successful response of your servers to PING?
How do you configure the targets that Prometheus should scrape?
What is the agent deployment mode of Prometheus?
questions.answer: agent mode is a light promtheus mode, which is focused for remote-write (remote storage), service-discovery and scraping specially for edge-computing/IoT and reducing for querying, alerting and local storage

questions.question: Which CLI command is suitable for unit testing Prometheus rules?
Which CLI command is suitable for checking validity of the config files?
How do you define the targets with SD that Prometheus should collect metrics from?
How can you delete the specific time series metrics of Prometheus?
questions.answer: starting the server with the flag --web.enable-admin-api + curl - X POST -g 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/admin/tsdb/delete_series?match[]={xxxx="yyy"}'

questions.question: How can you delete the all time series metrics of Prometheus?
questions.answer: starting the server with the flag --web.enable-admin-api + $ curl -X POST -g 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones'

questions.question: Which format does file-based SD provide?
What is PromQL?
What is histogram metric in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Histogramm samples observations (e.g. request durations or response sizes) and counts them in configurable buckets

questions.question: Which 4 data types are used in PromQL?
questions.answer: Scalar, String, Instant Vector, Range Vector

questions.question: What is the name of the vector in Prometheus that stores a single sample value?
Which PromQL function is used to estimate the value of a time series at a future time, t seconds from the current time, based on the range vector v?
Between what type of expressions can logical operators be defined?
Which function can be used to calculate the average of a range vector in Prometheus?
What is the diff between avg(...) and avg_over_time(...)?
questions.answer: avg_over_time(...) has range vector as input and returns range vector as output. avg(...) has instant vector as input and returns aggregated number.

questions.question: With which type of metrics is the rate(...) function primarily used in Prometheus?
What does the term “offset” refer to in Prometheus?
questions.answer: offset refers to the past time as duration

questions.question: What distinguishes the rate(...) and irate(...) query functions in Prometheus?
questions.answer: rate(...) calc avg rate of change of a time series over the specified time range, irate(...) calc avg rate of change of a time series at the last 2 data points

questions.question: What distinguishes the rate(...) and deriv(...) query functions in Prometheus?
questions.answer: deriv(...) operates on gauge and rate(...) operates on counter

questions.question: Which type of metric is suitable for measuring the internal temperature of a server?
What is the data type of Prometheus metric values?
How many unique series are generated by a histogram metric type?
What are the 4 components of the Prometheus metrics data model?
questions.answer: metric name, metrics label, timestamp, value

questions.question: What is the difference between the ceil and floor functions?
questions.answer: floor(...) = round a number down, ceil(...) = round a number up

questions.question: Which query function among the following returns a result of 1 in case the specified time series does not exist?
What is the logical/arithmetic/comparison binary operator?
questions.answer: logical => OR, AND, UNLESS, arithmetic => + - * / % ^, comparison => ==, !=, >, <, >=, <=

questions.question: What is the vector matching?
What is the group modifiers?
questions.answer: a part of vector matching. on, ignoring + group_left, group_right

questions.question: Which function is NOT using counter metrics? irate(), increase(), reset(), idelta(), avg(), rate()
How to calc the time in days until the LAST certificate expiration?
What is the dimensional aggregation?
What is the significance of the double underscore “__” before a label name?
questions.answer: The label is a reserved label

questions.question: What is the HTTP headers to establish by Prometheus during each scrape?

Does Prometheus need to perform any format conversion on the metrics returned by a monitored Linux machine?

What is the most suitable exporter for monitoring an HTTP web server endpoint to verify that it returns a 200 status code?
Which Prometheus exporter is recommended for monitoring network devices?
Which networking protocol does Prometheus utilize for performing scrapes?
What is the purpose of a Prometheus metrics registry?
questions.answer: Registry serves as a central repository for collecting, storing, and managing metrics

questions.question: What is the purpose or definition of a Prometheus exporter?
questions.answer: Exporter is responsible for collecting metrics from a specific system, application, or service and exposing them for Prometheus

questions.question: In what scenarios would you use the Blackbox Exporter?
questions.answer: Network Service Monitoring, Helth Check, Externe Monitoring

questions.question: How does Prometheus identify the scrape path for its targets?
Which endpoints allows blackbox probing?
questions.answer: Blackbox Exporter allows blackbox probing of endpoints over HTTP, HTTPS, DNS, TCP, ICMP, gRPC

questions.question: In a scenario where you have a dynamic etcd database containing scrape targets for Prometheus, how should you configure service discovery?

Which exporter is the most suitable for monitoring Scala metrics among the following options?
How to keep pushgateway job labels? normally there are overwritten
How does Prometheus scrape the last batch job push time?
What is the 3 types of service system?
Is there a way to deactivate a specific route in Alertmanager for a specific time frame?
questions.answer: attribute in the route time_intervals ex. time_intervals: [holidays, offhours]. mute_time_interval is DEPRECATED.

questions.question: What is considered a best practice when it comes to alerting in monitoring systems: focusing on alerting based on symptoms or alerting based on causes?
What is the meaning of “alert symptoms” and “alert causes” in the context of monitoring systems?
Which aspect, symptoms or causes, is more visible to customers in the context of an issue?
What is the good naming convention for the recoring rules?
What is the acknowledge-based throttling and Waht is the time-based throttling?
questions.answer: acknowledge-based = notifications for an alert are sent to the recipient only once until the alert is acknowledged or resolved

questions.question: What are the 3 statuses of a Prometheus alert?
questions.answer: time-based = timiting the rate of notifications based on a specific time interval (ex. goup_interval, scrape_interval)

questions.question: How can I use a PromQL query to retrieve the currently active alerts in Alertmanager?
What is the recording rules in Prometheus?
How to define the recording rules?
questions.answer: aggregate and filter metrics with PromQL and storing them into Prometheus DB

questions.question: Whas is the alert fatigue?
Which feature of Alertmanager is responsible for formatting and customizing the alerts?
questions.answer: Alert fatigue refers to a situation where individuals or teams become overwhelmed or desensitized by a large volume of alerts

questions.question: How can you configure Alertmanager to disable the grouping of alerts for a specific route effectively?
Which software is commonly used for visualizing Prometheus metrics?
What does the term “inhibiting” refer to in the context of Alertmanager?
What is the format used for defining alerting rules?
questions.answer: Inhibiting refers to a feature that allows certain alerts to be stopped or prevented from generating notifications for a specified duration of time

questions.question: What is the significance of the for attribute in a Prometheus alert rule?
How can you temporarily mute/snooze/suppress an alert during maintenance in Prometheus?
questions.answer: for allows for a delay or threshold before an alert is firing, helping to prevent false positives and reduce noise in alerting systems

questions.question: What is the name of Prometheus native dashboarding and visualization feature?
How can you coordinate the simultaneous sending of multiple alerts with similar label sets in Prometheus?
Which feature of Alertmanager is resonsilbe for sending alert to the right receiver?
What is the purpose of the repeat_interval/conitnue/group_wait/group_inteval attribute in an Alertmanager route configuration?
Which 2 attributes of an alerting rule can be used to include extra metadata?
questions.answer: repeat_interval: is used to determine the wait time before a firing alert that has already been successfully

questions.question: What are required for a high-availability configuration of Alertmanager?
questions.answer: continue: specifies whether to continue processing subsequent routes after sending a notification for an alert

questions.question: What are the 3 statuses of Alertmanager Silences?
questions.answer: group_wait: sets how long to initially wait to send a notification
